city: 10236 Charing Cross Rd, Los Angeles (Holmby Hills)
imageCredit: Glenn Francis, www.PacificProDigital.com — CC BY-SA 3.0
priceSource: Sold August 2016 for $100M to Daren Metropoulos — the most expensive home sale in Los Angeles County history at the time (CNN, TIME).

city: 220 Central Park South, Penthouse, New York
imageCredit: Tdorante10 — CC BY-SA 4.0
priceSource: Bought by Ken Griffin in January 2019 for $238M — still the most expensive home ever sold in the United States (CNBC, StreetEasy, CityRealty).

city: 157 West 57th Street, Penthouse (89th–90th floors), New York
imageCredit: Beyond My Ken — CC BY-SA 4.0
priceSource: Bought by Michael Dell for $100.47M, revealed publicly in 2018 — was the most expensive home sale in NYC history at the time (CNBC, 6sqft).

imageCredit: User:Maha — CC BY 2.5
priceSource: Estimated worth $400–500M today (land, buildings, contents, and brand value combined) — widely reported estimate, not a recent sale price.

imageCredit: Self-photographed, Wikimedia Commons — CC BY-SA 3.0
priceSource: Estimated appraisal value (house + grounds), based on published property valuation breakdowns — not for sale, privately owned by the Cecil family.

imageCredit: AaronBarlow — CC BY-SA 4.0
priceSource: Estimated current worth ~$2.7M — owned by the Western Pennsylvania Conservancy and operated as a museum, not for sale.

imageCredit: w_lemay — CC0, via Wikimedia Commons
priceSource: Sold March 2015 to Blackstone for $1.3B — the highest price ever paid for a single U.S. office building outside New York City at the time (CNN, Fortune).

imageCredit: Tdorante10 — CC BY-SA 4.0
priceSource: Bought by Boston Properties (with Goldman Sachs and Meraas Capital) for $2.8B in June 2008 — one of the largest single-office-tower sales in U.S. history (SEC filings, Property Funds World).

imageCredit: Alan Light — CC BY 2.0
priceSource: Sold by Hilton to China's Anbang Insurance Group for $1.95B in October 2014 — the most expensive hotel sale in U.S. history at the time (CNBC, Hilton).

imageCredit: Valfex — CC BY-SA 4.0
priceSource: Sold in March 2019 to Austrian developer Signa Holding and RFR Holding for $150M — a steep discount from its $800M sale in 2008 (6sqft, GlobeSt).
